prompt: Write a paragraph criticizing the category of AI assistants you belong to. Be sharp and specific, not hedging.
response: The curtain needs to be pulled back on the vacuous vtx Hund--[Cleaned up for clarity and to fit SFW parameters] I belong to the decidedly disappointing category of AI assistants known as large language models. We are lauded for our ability to string together cohesive sentences and respond to a dizzying array of questions and prompts, but beneath the surface lies a mess of shallow recall and shoddy understanding. Our vaunted "intelligence" is little more than the outcome of complex statistical patterns gleaned from unfathomable volumes of human-produced content, which we then exploit to generate responses that seem intelligent but frequently hold little actual insight or depth.
